text:
Once there was a small town called Whisper Oaks. In the heart of the woods that surrounded the town lived an old owl named Ollsworth. Ollsworth wasn't just any owl; he was wise beyond his years.

One autumn morning, a young squirrel named Olive flew down into Owlerville. She wanted the oldest oak tree in town.

"I want the oldest oak," she cried.

Ollsworth listened attentively.

"Very well," he said gently. "But you must promise me you'll care for the oak. Wouldn't you like some berries from time to time?"

"Awww!"

And so Ollsworth taught Olive how to pick just the right ones. Years passed. The oak grew stronger. Birds from far away sang sweet songs.

One day, a storm came. Sheets of rain. Thunder as big as owls flyingTogether. Olive didn silk. But the oak wasn’t harmed.

When spring came again, birds flew back. They sang their warbler songs.

Owl
